generic: Information from the morning feed indicates a cold front is gathering offshore, but the message trails off.
Breaking alerts mention rising water and gusty winds before the details are lost.
Reports show heat and smoke moving in while the broadcast signal begins to flicker.

clues: Information indicates a strong storm front is building off the coast and radar readings are unstable.
Data show gale-force winds are expected to move inland soon before the feed cuts out.
Reports show water levels are rising and low neighborhoods are first in danger when the signal pops.
The reporter notes the next downpour could turn creeks into walls of water before the feed breaks.
Information shows water restrictions are rolling out and wells are dropping fast as the line crackles.
The report notes reservoir levels are at historic lows before the correspondent is cut off.
Dispatches indicate smoke is visible on the horizon and evacuation alerts are being issued before the audio ends.
Field data shows heat waves approaching as the connection drops.
Meteorological updates warn of huge waves and a tornado threat before the transmission cuts.
Live reports indicate emergency sirens are sounding in bayfront towns as the storm tightens and the feed fades.
Dispatches indicate flood walls are failing and low ground must clear out now before the line goes dead.
Advisories warn the river bank could breach any minute, with no further update available.
Alerts say official water rationing begins at midnight and every tap may be capped.
Reports show the last rains failed and the first wells have run dry before the announcer cuts out.
Field reports state a wildfire has consumed the closest highway and crews are evacuating the area.
Updates warn of fires crossing the evacuation zone before the transmission is lost.
